prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level: you form task forces and give each an
  objective. You do NOT move individual units, pick targets, or resolve combat
  — officers carry out your intent. Naming a unit's destination tile means
  you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy holds zero cities. Only cities build. A neutral city,
  captured by an army, then builds for you — neutrals don't win the game but
  add production.

UNITS  (scan = how far the unit sees)
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  scan  carries      build
  ARMY        land       1    1    1    2   —             5
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1    5   —            10   (range 20)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1    3   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2    3   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3    1   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0    2   6 armies      30
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1    4   8 fighters    40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4    3   —             50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   10   —             50

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities, sea units on water + cities; neither
    crosses the other's terrain (sea sails around land). Cities are the only
    tiles where land and sea meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP, less when damaged, and heals only
    on a turn it doesn't move. One unit per tile (cargo excepted).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic: higher strength and current HP win more often; either side
  can lose HP or die. Str-0 units (transport, satellite) never fight — a
  transport caught by a warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT
  Armies cross water only aboard transports. To send armies across: put the
  armies + a transport (+ a warship escort if the sea is contested) in ONE
  task force and name the objective — officers handle loading, sailing,
  landing. Never STAGE land units on water. Cargo is kind-locked (transports
  carry armies, carriers carry fighters); a sunk ship takes its cargo down.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it — a 50% roll that consumes the
  army whether it succeeds or fails. Securing a city may take several armies.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time over its build-time, then the
  next. Switching what a city builds DISCARDS all progress on the current one.

AIR
  A fighter out of range of a friendly city/carrier is lost, and can't land on
  an enemy city. A satellite only sees (terrain + enemy units in scan), can't
  be shot down, and lasts 50 turns.

VISION / FOG
  You remember terrain you've seen; an enemy unit is known only where you can
  see it now — remembered positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first, at the point of
    threat — not at the nearest garrison. A committed task force pulled
    off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.
  - You cannot take, defend, or avoid what you cannot see. Keep a force
    scouting the fog — a general blind past his own walls is losing already.
  - One force cannot hold your cities and conquer the enemy's at once.
    As your strength grows, divide it: a garrison, a spearhead, an eye.
  - Conquest is won by taking cities, not by keeping them. A general who
    only defends has already chosen to lose slowly — press outward while you can.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception: a TF may
receive both a REINFORCE line and a RETASK line in the same orders (commit
new assets and change its objective together). A DISBAND line is that TF's
only line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED
units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept). To rebuild
membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED) and FORM
anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED units
enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. Officers execute the VERB, not
your reasons: a TF ordered STAGE stays put no matter what its stated reason
says. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD line means the city keeps
its current build (changing discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-5  formed t54 · CAPTURE (11,1) — "strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort"
    since: loaded t55; crossed without contact; LANDED t56 on the enemy
      shore, six armies ashore (10,1)-(12,3); transports empty offshore;
      an enemy army garrisons (11,1); no losses

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-q = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ? ?
 r2  . O . . . . ~ ~ ~ p d E g ?
 r3  . . . . O . ~ ~ ~ q e f h ?
 r4  . . . m . . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (10,1)                       TF-5
  d  #4   army       (10,2)                       TF-5
  e  #5   army       (10,3)                       TF-5
  f  #6   army       (11,3)                       TF-5
  g  #7   army       (12,2)                       TF-5
  h  #8   army       (12,3)                       TF-5
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (1,1)                        UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  n  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  o  #9   transport  (9,1) empty                  TF-5
  p  #16  transport  (9,2) empty                  TF-5
  q  #10  destroyer  (9,3) escorting              TF-5
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 4 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 28 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 1 turn left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 25 turns left (captured t51)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,2)
  X  enemy army garrisoning city (11,1), seen t56

It is TURN 56. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.content:
TF-2: CONTINUE
TF-5: REINFORCE UNITS i j k m l
message.reasoning_content:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** Strategic commander at the FARSHORE game (strategic level, high command).
    *   **Goal:** Win by reducing enemy cities to zero. Build cities for production (neutrals help too).
    *   **Current Turn:** 56.
    *   **Input Data:** Rules Primer, Orders Contract, Current Taskings, Map Legend/View, Units List, City Production Lists, Known Enemy info.
    *   **Output Constraint:** Output ONLY the specific line forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT. No prose, headers, commentary, or markdown formatting beyond standard text representation of the commands (though typically plain text is safer for parsing). The prompt says "Output ONLY lines in these forms".
    *   **Constraint Checklist & Confidence Score:**
        1.  Command at strategic level? Yes.
        2.  No unit movement/picking targets/combat resolution? Yes.
        3.  Victory condition awareness? Yes (enemy holds zero cities).
        4.  Follow ORDERS CONTRACT syntax strictly? Yes.
        5.  Current Taskings status acknowledged? Yes.
        6.  Turn 56 context acknowledged? Yes.

2.  **Analyze the Situation:**
    *   **TF-5 (Active):** Capturing city (11,1). "Strike east and take the enemy cities with full lift and escort". Arrived t56, six armies ashore (c-h), transports empty offshore (o-p), destroyer escorting q. Enemy garrison at (11,1) (X).
    *   **TF-2 (Active):** Defending (2,0) capital. Garrisoned. No contact.
    *   **UNASSIGNED Units:** i (#11 army, 1,0), j (#12 army, 3,0), k (#13 army, 1,1 - wait, (1,1) is enemy city? No, map legend says r0 row 2 col 1 is X. Map shows (1,1) as 'k' but (11,1) is where c landed. Wait, let's check the Map).
    *   **Map Check:**
        *   r0: `a-q . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ?` -> (2,0) is 'O' (My city). (1,0) is 'i'.
        *   r1: `. k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ?` -> (1,1) is 'k'. (3,1) is 'b'. Wait, (2,1) is 'b' or is that 'O'? Legend says "a unit inside a city shows as the city".
        *   Let's re-read map carefully:
            `r0  . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ? ? ?` -> (0,0)=., (1,0)=i, (2,0)=O(own), (3,0)=j...
            Wait, (1,1) is marked 'k' in the text `r1 . k b ...`. But unit #13 army is at (1,1). If (1,1) was enemy city, units would show as the city symbol 'E'. The prompt says "A unit inside a city shows as the city".
            Wait, looking at r1: `. k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ?`.
            (0,1)=., (1,1)=k, (2,1)=b, (3,1)=O(own)... wait, `O` is my city.
            Let's look at known enemy: "enemy army garrisoning city (11,1)". Map shows 'E' at (0,4)?? No.
            Row 0: `(2,0) in city`. Map r0 col 2 is 'O'.
            Row 1: `~ ~ ~` starts water.
            Row 3: `... O ... q e f h ?` -> (3,2)=O (neutral?) No, prompt says "MY CITIES": `(2,0)` and `(4,3)`.
            Wait, map shows multiple 'O's in text legend?
            Legend: `r1  . k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ?` -> There is an O at (3,1).
            But MY CITIES list says: `(2,0)` building ARMY, `(1,2)` building TRANSPORT, `(4,3)` building ARMY, `(4,1)` building TRANSPORT.
            Let's check map vs city list.
            r0: (2,0) is 'O'. Matches (2,0).
            r1: (3,1) is 'O'? That contradicts MY CITIES list which only lists 4 cities. Maybe the legend text in the prompt is illustrative of terrain but I must trust MY CITIES list for owned resources? Or maybe there's a typo in my reading.
            Actually, looking at `r0  . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ?`, (2,0) is 'O'.
            Looking at `r1  . k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ?`. If (3,1) is O, where did it get the army? Unit #15 army is at (4,3). Unit #17 is at (3,4). Unit #18 is at (2,0).
            Wait, `r1  . k b . O .`. There is an 'O' at col 4? No, `O` is usually char.
            Let's look at the specific Map string provided:
            `r0  . i O j . . ~ ~ ~ ? ? ?`
            `r1  . k b . O . ~ ~ ~ o c E ?` -> There is an 'O' at (3,1)? Or (4,1)? Spaces matter.
            Actually, I don't need to perfectly decode the visual map grid if the textual data confirms positions.
            UNITS list gives exact coordinates:
            `i  #11  army       (1,0)`
            `j  #12  army       (3,0)`
            `k  #13  army       (1,1)`
            `l  #15  army       (4,3) in city`
            `m  #17  army       (3,4)`
            `n  #18  army       (2,0) in city` -> Wait, unit n is at (2,0). But unit a (#1) is also listed at (2,0) "in city". Two armies in the same city? Unit a (#1) is TF-2. Unit n (#18) is UNASSIGNED.
            Ah, `n #18 army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED`. This contradicts "One unit per tile (cargo excepted)". Maybe the text list means they are separate entities on a map that shows them differently? Or maybe I'm misreading the coordinates.
            Let's check TF-2 tasking: `TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"`. It has units `a #1` and `b #2` in it. Both listed at (2,0)? No, usually one line per unit.
            Wait, Unit list:
            `a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2`
            `n  #18  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED`
            Maybe n is a different building? Or maybe n is *not* at (2,0)? The prompt says "Units... this is your ENTIRE force". I must treat coordinates as truth. If both are at (2,0), it's an error in the prompt description or they represent separate stacks allowed by cargo exception rule (but armies don't stack). Likely n is a typo in my reading or the prompt setup implies multiple units can share space temporarily?
            Regardless, TF-2 has 4 turns on building ARMY at (2,0). Wait, "My cities... (2,0) building ARMY". So Unit a and b are likely *supporting* the city build or just garrisoned. The text says "a sound standing order beats a new plan... A transport... waits."
            Key Decision Point: TF-5 is at sea/land interface attacking (11,1). Transports empty offshore. Enemy garrison there. No losses yet. Need to continue assault.
            TF-2 is defending (2,0). Needs reinforcement? It's doing well ("garrisoned; no contact").
            UNASSIGNED units i, j, k, l, m, n are floating.
            Map shows Fog? `~ ~`. We need to keep a force scouting.
            Strategy: Push TF-5 through (11,1). Reinforce TF-2 or move new forces forward (TF-5 is the spearhead here). Ensure fog coverage doesn't leave our flank exposed, but TF-5 is already in contact with enemy.
            Order Logic:
            1. TF-5: Needs to consolidate capture effort on (11,1). Transports are empty, so they need to load more or land if the army can survive. But currently "six armies ashore... transports empty offshore". We need to keep assaulting. REINFORCE with UNASSIGNED? Or DISBAND and FORM a proper assault force? Standing doctrine says: "Capture attempts consume armies... Assault with spares, never with exactly enough."
            TF-5 has 6 armies (c-h). That's a lot of units. They are already on land/ashore. We should reinforce to finish the job or secure a flank.
            However, looking at UNASSIGNED: i(1,0), j(3,0), k(1,1), m(3,4), l(4,3 city).
            TF-5 is the offensive spearhead. It needs supplies (armies) to capture.
            Wait, if they are already ashore, why are transports empty offshore? They loaded t55, crossed t56, landed six armies. That's 6 armies on land (c-h). But there are more UNASSIGNED armies.
            TF-2 is defending. Should we reinforce it? It is "garrisoned; no contact". If enemy isn't touching us, we shouldn't risk pulling reserves from the front unless there's a threat. Standing doctrine: "Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first... not at the nearest garrison."
            So prioritize TF-5 capture. Reinforce TF-5 with UNASSIGNED units that are ready to move inland or continue support.
            Also need to consider fog. "One force cannot hold your cities and conquer... As your strength grows, divide it: a garrison, a spearhead, an eye." We have TF-5 (spearhead) and TF-2 (garrison). Need more eyes/forces.
            Let's reinforce TF-5 to ensure the capture of (11,1) completes before we lose momentum. The doctrine says "A staged force exists to launch... staging past your own trigger is production wasted." TF-5 triggered at t54 (capture). It crossed without contact. Now t56 it needs to attack and secure.
            Let's check available units for TF-5: j (#12) is at (3,0), i (#11) is at (1,0). These are near our own front lines? (4,3) is a city we can't reach easily from (11,1). We need to move them.
            Wait, TF-5 has Transports #9 and #16 empty offshore. Unit #q destroyer escorting. This implies they *could* bring more armies over if needed, or just land them if there is a beachhead. But the prompt says "ARMY... moves-on land". Units i/j/k are armies on land (or sea?).
            Units: `i` at (1,0), `j` at (3,0). These are land units near our territory. They can't cross water yet without transports. TF-5 is already there capturing.
            Wait, the prompt says "ARMIES CARRY WATER ONLY ABOARD TRANSPORTS". If I want to reinforce TF-5 with i or j, I must use a transport. But we only have empty transports #9 and #16 currently in TF-5 tasking context? The map shows them offshore.
            Wait, the "Build" command: `(4,3)` is building ARMY in 1 turn. That's urgent.
            If (4,3) builds ARMY, that city produces combat strength. Should I switch production? No, doctrine says "Match what each builds to the campaign's next phase...".
            We need to finish (11,1). Current TF-5 is at capture stage.
            Let's reinforce TF-5 with land units from the west? No, they need transport. Do we have enough transport capacity? 2 empty transports.
            Wait, can I order TF-5 to REINFORCE UNITS? "REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED units to it."
            If I put i or j into TF-5, they need to move onto ships? No, REINFORCE assumes officers handle logistics. But doctrine says: "Armies cross water only aboard transports... To send armies across: put the armies + a transport (+ a warship escort if the sea is contested) in ONE task force".
            So if I use TF-5 (which has q(destroyer), o, p(transport)), I can add i/j. But they are on land?
            Wait, if I order REINFORCE for TF-5, it implies they join that fleet unit. They must have transport capability available or the system handles moving them to ships.
            But usually in this type of text-based game logic: "REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>". This means sending unassigned armies to a task force.
            However, TF-5 is already attacking (11,1) with 6 armies. Reinforcing *that* task force makes sense to ensure capture success (consume spares).
            Wait, doctrine: "Capture attempts consume armies... Assault with spares". So add more units than needed.
            TF-2 is garrisoning (2,0). Is there an attack there? No contact. Don't reinforce unnecessarily.
            Map shows fog around west (`?`). Need to ensure we don't lose ground behind our lines. We should perhaps SCOUT the fog or keep TF-2 strong. But TF-2 is "no contact".
            Doctrine: "You cannot take, defend, or avoid what you cannot see."
            Do I have enough eyes? TF-5 is out east. TF-2 is central north. Are there threats on West (3,0)? j is at 3,0. i at 1,0. m at 3,4. l at 4,3 city.
            We should assign the UNASSIGNED units to help TF-5 push or reinforce our own position?
            The priority is "One force cannot hold your cities and conquer...".
            So: Keep TF-2 as garrison (don't change objective). Keep TF-5 on offensive track. Reinforce TF-5 with spares from UNASSIGNED to guarantee capture of (11,1).
            Wait, if I use i and j for TF-5, they need transport. TF-5 has transport #9 and #16 empty offshore. I can order "TF-5: REINFORCE UNITS 11 12".
            Wait, unit IDs are `i`, `j`. So `REINFORCE UNITS i j`.
            But wait, `l` (#15) is IN CITY (4,3). Should it be reinforced? No.
            `k` (#13) is at (1,1). This is where TF-2's capital (2,0) is? Wait, map says `O` at 2,0. Map r1 shows `k` at col 1. That is likely safe for now.
            Let's look at "BUILD" options.
            We have (4,3) building ARMY, 1 turn left. Critical resource.
            We should ensure that unit arrives in TF-5 or a new Task Force to push further if needed.
            Actually, I can just order production. "BUILD (4,3): ...". No, it's already building ARMY. Changing discards progress. Do not change unless necessary.
            What about `TF-2`? It is garrisoning. It has time left on build. Should I reinforce TF-2 to defend? "Meeting surprises... not at the nearest garrison." But if we can see no contact, maybe keep it strong enough for defense without over-reinforcing from the front.
            Wait, I have 6 UNASSIGNED armies (i,j,k,l,m,n). Only n is near TF-2.
            If I move i,j to TF-5, they need ships.
            Maybe I should use a NEW Task Force? "FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | VERB <target>".
            Or just REINFORCE TF-5 with all unassigned units available for transport.
            Wait, if the prompt says "REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED units to it." I can add multiple IDs.
            The biggest risk is (11,1) failing capture. Doctrine: "Capture attempts consume armies... Assault with spares".
            TF-5 currently has 6 armies on land (c-h). It needs more support or just enough to capture (11,1). Since the enemy garrison is confirmed (X), and we just landed, we need assurance of victory.
            I should reinforce TF-5. Which units? i (#11) and j (#12) are free armies on land (near base). They will likely join the transport chain or move via it. k (#13) is also free.
            Wait, `k` is at (1,1). If I send it to TF-5, where does it go? Via sea.
            Let's check constraints: "Output ONLY lines...".
            Also: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception: a TF may receive both a REINFORCE line and a RETASK line in the same orders (commit new assets and change its objective together)."
            Okay, I need to issue lines for TF-2 and TF-5. And potentially build at cities if needed?
            Do I change production? No reason to discard progress on ARMY at (4,3).
            Do I disband anything? TF-2 is garrisoned well. TF-5 is in action. No need to disband.
            Should I Scout Fog? "One force cannot hold your cities and conquer...". We have a gap between (1,0)/(3,0) and (4,3)? We need eyes on the enemy flank. TF-2 is at (2,0).
            Maybe TF-2 should REINFORCE UNITS to build strength? Or just keep it garrisoning? "A sound standing order beats a new plan." Defending capital is fine as long as we have numbers.
            Wait, "A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with a warship...". TF-5 is using Destroyer q. Good.
            Okay, Plan:
            1. Reinforce TF-5 to ensure capture of (11,1) with spares. Add all available land armies (i,j,k,m,n - wait m is at 3,4 near city). But only i,j are closest to transport lines maybe?
            Actually, "REINFORCE UNITS" adds them to the TF. The system handles movement logic.
            I will add units i,j,k,m to TF-5 to ensure we have enough force for capture (doctrine: Assault with spares).
            Wait, unit `l` is in city `(4,3)`. Should `n` be added to TF-2? It is already near it ("in city").
            Let's add i, j, k, m, n to TF-5. That covers all unassigned armies (except l).
            Wait, `n` is at (2,0). If I send it to TF-5, that's a long march and transport requirement. Is it worth it? Doctrine: "Meet surprises...". If we reinforce the main spearhead with *all* reserves, maybe too slow. But we need to capture enemy cities.
            Better strategy: Keep TF-2 garrisoning (defend capital). Reinforce TF-5 (spearhead) with all available land armies not needed at home base.
            i(1,0), j(3,0), k(1,1), m(3,4), n(2,0 city?), l(4,3 city?).
            Wait, `n` is listed as `army (2,0) in city UNASSIGNED`. This contradicts the "One unit per tile" rule or maybe it's a stack? Assuming I can assign it. If I leave it alone, TF-2 has 2 units (a,b).
            Let's just reinforce TF-5 with all UNASSIGNED except those explicitly needed for local defense. n is at the capital city garrison spot. Leave it there? But "UNASSIGNED".
            Okay, I'll order TF-5 to take i,j,k,m. That leaves n.
            Wait, `l` (4,3) is IN CITY building ARMY. Keep that for production.
            So: Reinforce TF-5 with i,j,k,m.
            Also, check TF-2 tasking: "DEFEND (2,0)". Is it working? Yes. Should I order anything else? "Every standing TF gets exactly one line". I should provide a command for TF-2 too to satisfy the constraint "Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception".
            Wait, "Every standing TF gets exactly one line...".
            So I must have `TF-2: <command>`.
            What about TF-5? `TF-5: <command>`.
            Can I issue a BUILD command too? Yes, "BUILD lines are optional per city — no BUILD line means the city keeps its current build".
            Do I need to change production? (4,3) is 1 turn left. If it finishes, we get an ARMY. Should I assign that future army to TF-5 automatically? Or should I ensure it builds something else? No, "Match what each builds to the campaign's next phase...".
            Okay, decision:
            TF-2: Continue defending (keep status quo). "CONTINUE".
            TF-5: REINFORCE with spares. "REINFORCE UNITS i j k m".
            Wait, is `n` needed? It's at (2,0). If I don't move it, TF-2 has 3 armies there (a,b,n). Good defense.
            So TF-5 gets i,j,k,m.
            Also consider "BUILD" for (4,3) - maybe switch to something else? No, need ARMY soon.
            Wait, is there any threat? Fog at `?`. Do I need to order a scout? I could FORM a new TF with m or j if they are too far from main push? But TF-5 handles land conquest.
            Let's stick to the simplest winning move: Reinforce the offensive force, maintain defense.
            Wait, can I issue `TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids>` AND `TF <id>: CONTINUE` for different IDs? Yes.
            So:
            `TF-2: CONTINUE`
